2603.29693.title: Measuring the metacognition of AI
2603.29693.paper_summary: A robust decision-making process must take into account uncertainty, especially when the choice involves inherent risks. Because artificial Intelligence (AI) systems are increasingly integrated into decision-making workflows, managing uncertainty relies more and more on the metacognitive capabilities of these systems; i.e, their ability to assess the reliability of and regulate their own decisions. Hence, it is crucial to employ robust methods to measure the metacognitive abilities of AI. This paper is primarily a methodological contribution arguing for the adoption of the meta-d' framework, or its model-free alternatives, as the gold standard for assessing the metacognitive sensitivity of AIs--the ability to generate confidence ratings that distinguish correct from incorrect responses. Moreover, we propose to leverage signal detection theory (SDT) to measure the ability of AIs to spontaneously regulate their decisions based on uncertainty and risk. To demonstrate the practical utility of these psychophysical frameworks, we conduct two series of experiments on three large language models (LLMs)--GPT-5, DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp, and Mistral-Medium-2508. In the first experiments, LLMs performed a primary judgment followed by a confidence rating. In the second, LLMs only performed the primary judgment, while we manipulated the risk associated with either response. On the one hand, applying the meta-d' framework allows us to conduct comparisons along three axes: comparing an LLM to optimality, comparing different LLMs on a given task, and comparing the same LLM across different tasks. On the other hand, SDT allows us to assess whether LLMs become more conservative when risks are high.
2603.29693.paper_summary_zh: 一個健全的決策過程必須考慮不確定性，尤其當選擇涉及固有風險時。由於人工智慧（AI）系統越來越多地融入決策工作流程，管理不確定性越來越依賴這些系統的元認知能力；即它們評估和調節自身決策的可靠性的能力。因此，採用健全的方法來測量AI的元認知能力至關重要。本文主要是一項方法論貢獻，主張採用meta-d'框架或其無模型替代方案作為評估AI元認知敏感性的黃金標準——即生成區分正確與錯誤反應的信心評分的能力。此外，我們提出利用信號檢測理論（SDT）來測量AI根據不確定性和風險自發調節決策的能力。為了展示這些心理物理框架的實用性，我們對三個大型語言模型（LLMs）進行了兩系列實驗——GPT-5、DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp和Mistral-Medium-2508。在第一次實驗中，LLMs進行了主要判斷，隨後給出了信心評分。在第二次實驗中，LLMs僅進行主要判斷，而我們操縱了與任一反應相關的風險。一方面，應用meta-d'框架使我們能夠沿三個軸進行比較：將LLM與最佳性進行比較、在特定任務上比較不同的LLM，以及在不同任務上比較相同的LLM。另一方面，SDT使我們能夠評估當風險較高時LLMs是否變得更加保守。
2603.29693.author: Richard Servajean et.al.
